# Step 4


## Task – Step 4 - Program production

●	Read book Step 4: Program production (pp 435- 476)

## Task – Program plan

●	Refine program structure by organizing methods and applications in a program plan that describes who must do what and when, considering available channels and the delivery systems. The program structure should describe the following:

 - the themes (general organizing constructs) (p.356)  
 - the scope of the program (the breadth and amount of the program) (p. 358)  
 - the sequence (the order) of program activities (p. 358)  
 - the delivery vehicles/channels (questions are: what, when, where, how much, how often?) (p. 359)  
 - Also think about developmental stages. The book emphasizes cultural relevance (p. 438), but as Development and Health psychology students you should  always be aware of age-relevant issues and developmental stages.


## Task

●	Prepare plans for program materials: Create specifications and working documents. 
  - Working documents express the themes and messages of the materials.Points to consider: type of application, length, feasibility, salience, production qualities, design, clarity, etc). 

Specify the necessary creative resources (program producers) so that you can produce the applications. 
(Remember that you have to collaborate with experts in different professions and that you are the only expert aware of the parameters of the methods; check the applications for parameters!)

Example format for your program material plans:

|Program Components | Description | Producers |
| --- | --- | --- | 
| .......... | .......... | .......... | 
| .......... | .......... | .......... |


## Task - Draft messages, materials and protocols

## Task - Pretest, refine, and produce materials. 

●	How would you pretest the program applications and materials?
o	Pretest materials on members of the target population and professionals
o	Evaluate the results 
o	Anticipate implementation before you finalize the applications and program
o	Check: Are all your change objectives still in the final program?

11-13h 

## Step 5 - Planning program adoption and implementation

Chapter 8 is relevant here


Step 5 is about how to implement the program. Who has to do what to keep the program up and running. You want the program that you have developed to be used widely, you want it to be used the way you intended it to be used and you hope it will be used for quite some time. It is therefore very important that potential program users, or implementers, are actually aware of the existence of the program, are motivated to use it, understand how to use it, etc. Consequently, you will have to repeat the steps again, but now for the program users (who are now your target group). 

15-17H LITTLE GROUPS 
●	Use this time to finish working on task 4.1-4.5 
●	Prepare your final group presentation for tomorrow morning, covering step 1 to 5.
